name: Gallinera Valley and La Llacuna Loop from Gandía
name_original: El Vall de la Gallinera y La Llacuna — Vuelta desde Gandía
description: This difficult 37.4-mile road cycling route from Gandía explores the scenic Vall de la Gallinera and La Llacuna with 3084 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Road cycling around Gandia offers a diverse landscape, ranging from coastal flatlands to challenging mountainous terrain. The region features well-maintained roads along the Mediterranean coast and inland routes through the Valencian countryside. Cyclists can explore greenways, river routes, and wetland areas, providing varied scenery and surfaces. This geography caters to different skill levels, from leisurely rides to demanding climbs. Best road cycling routes around Gandia The most popular road cycling route is El Monduver — Circular from Gandia through La Drova and Barx ports, a 37.2 miles (59.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant climbing through mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Gallinera Valley and La Llacuna Loop from Gandía, a difficult 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route offers scenic mountain roads with sustained ascents and descents. Local road cyclists also love the Tour of the interior of La Safor, La Costera and La Vall d'Albaida, a 50.3 miles (81.0 km) trail leading through the Valencian countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Gandia is defined by coastal flatlands, mountainous terrain, and greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to advanced mountain climbs. The routes in Gandia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 13,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Gandia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Gandia?
A: Gandia offers a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll find coastal flatlands with well-maintained roads and stunning Mediterranean views, as well as challenging mountainous terrain with steep climbs and technical descents in areas like the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park or the Sierra de Falconera. There are also inland routes through the Valencian countryside with a mix of flat and gently rolling terrain, and unique greenways like the Via Verde de la Safor.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Gandia?
A: Yes, Gandia caters to all skill levels. For leisurely rides, the coastal flatlands provide accessible terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859977417/the-costa-del-levante-circular-from-gandia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Costa del Levante — Circular from Gandia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that offers coastal scenery. Additionally, the Via Verde de la Safor is a traffic-free, mostly flat, and well-paved route, ideal for families or those seeking an easier ride through orange groves and Mediterranean vegetation.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders seeking a challenge will find plenty of options in Gandia's mountainous areas. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839457779/el-monduver-circular-from-gandia-through-la-drova-and-barx-ports" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Monduver — Circular from Gandia through La Drova and Barx ports&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839698228/gallinera-valley-and-la-llacuna-loop-from-gandia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley and La Llacuna Loop from Gandía&lt;/a&gt; feature significant climbing through mountain passes and scenic mountain roads with sustained ascents and descents.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Gandia?
A: The period from October to May is especially ideal for cycling in Gandia. The region enjoys over 300 days of sunshine and an average annual temperature of 18°C, providing favorable conditions for outdoor activities throughout these months.

Q: What kind of natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Gandia's routes offer diverse natural beauty. You can cycle along stunning Mediterranean coastlines, through lush orange groves, and past Mediterranean vegetation. Inland routes traverse the Valencian countryside, while mountainous areas provide rugged terrain and spectacular views. The Marjal de Gandia wetland area also offers tranquil routes where you can observe diverse flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to visit while cycling?
A: Yes, you can incorporate cultural exploration into your rides. Gandia's historic center is home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducal Palace of the Borgias&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4027411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bairén Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5813634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Santa Anna&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's rich past.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Gandia?
A: There are over 90 road cycling routes available around Gandia, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in the Gandia area?
A: Yes, Gandia offers several long-distance routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859615356/tour-of-the-interior-of-la-safor-la-costera-and-la-vall-d-albaida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the interior of La Safor, La Costera and La Vall d'Albaida&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 81 km (50.3 miles) trail that takes you through the Valencian countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24950524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moraira Beach – Ascent to Montgó from Dénia loop from Gandia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 120 km.

Q: Are there any specific cycling events in Gandia?
A: Gandia hosts cycling events, such as the ALE Cycling Gandía, which is part of the UCI Cycling for All calendar. These events often feature routes that combine the Mediterranean surroundings with mountainous terrain on quiet secondary roads.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Gandia?
A: The road cycling routes in Gandia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 13,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Gandia's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic coastal rides.

Q: Are there any public transport options to access cycling routes in Gandia?
A: Gandia is well-connected, and public transport options are available to reach the city, which serves as a starting point for many routes. For specific trailheads, local bus services might be available, but it's advisable to check local transport schedules for routes that extend further into the countryside or mountains.

Q: Are there any permits required for cycling on certain routes or in natural parks?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads and designated greenways in the Gandia area. However, if you plan to venture into specific natural parks or protected areas, it's always a good idea to check local regulations, though road cycling typically uses established routes that don't require special permissions.
tips: One of the most attractive and demanding stretches of road between La Safor and Vall d'Albaida. From Villalonga uphill to Llacuna, the road offers an entertaining zig-zag route with beautiful views in all directions.
It is a beautiful valley formed by eight towns that are aligned on the boulevard. It is a very popular area among hikers, as there is a very extensive network of paths in the area.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult road ride, requiring very good fitness. It features significant climbs, particularly the demanding zig-zag ascent from Villalonga up to La Llacuna.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on firm, well-paved surfaces suitable for road bikes, offering good grip. However, be aware that some segments might be unpaved or difficult, potentially requiring you to push your bike for short stretches.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience beautiful views in all directions, especially along the stretch between Villalonga and La Llacuna. Key highlights include the Beautiful section between Villalonga and La Llacuna and the stunning Gallinera Valley, known for its lush mountains, picturesque villages, and fields of cherry and almond trees.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring or autumn for pleasant temperatures. Visiting in March is particularly recommended to witness the spectacular cherry trees in bloom, which paint the valley slopes white.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact start of this route from Gandía are not provided, Gandía offers various parking options. For the Vall de Gallinera area, you would typically look for parking within one of the villages or designated areas if you plan to start directly there.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route through El Vall de la Gallinera y La Llacuna.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' due to its significant climbs and distance. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: Does the route pass through any villages or towns where I can find amenities?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route traverses the Vall de Gallinera, which is comprised of eight picturesque villages. These villages, such as Benirrama, Benialí, and Alpatró, offer glimpses into rural charm and history, and you can often find small cafes or restaurants for refreshments.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and elevation, you should pack essentials like sufficient water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire for varying temperatures, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Consider layers, especially for descents or cooler mornings.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. Notably, it covers parts of the Via Verda de la Safor, El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, and the Camino del Alba.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenging climbs to be aware of?
faqs.answer: The climb from Villalonga up to La Llacuna is a particularly attractive and demanding section, featuring an entertaining zig-zag route. The climb out of Pego also offers increasingly better views as you ascend.

waypoints.name: Beautiful section between Villalonga and La Llacuna
